entries.title: About the Course
entries.content: Complete assigned models, focusing on craftsmanship. 1st-Year Models include simple boxes and envelopes based mainly on the square or rectangular prism.

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: Grade 2 students who are new to sloyd should take Sloyd: Intro and then continue the courses in sequence.

Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

Sloyd: Level 1
☐ View the "Sloyd Introduction" video for an overview of materials and resources used in Sloyd (see Quick Links below).

☐ Purchase supplies and organize them so they are easily accessible.

☐ Read through pages 1-7 in the book to gain a general idea of the purpose and how to go about the lessons. This will also give you an understanding of how you can adapt supplies to fit our time. For example, "Gummed Parquetry" is equivalent to 1" circle label stickers, and "Gummed Stars" are similar to gold foil star stickers. You can also substitute scotch tape or stapling in some places for pasting and threading. (i.e., on the "wall pocket" you can tape the pieces together where they join as opposed to stitching.) 

☐ Keep the page of images for each of the first-year models for reference while working on models (see Appendices).

☐ Allow time for loading and viewing videos. We recommend teachers preview tutorials and work a model before teaching it.

☐ Before each lesson, gather needed materials as listed at the beginning of each day's lesson plan. 

☐ Introduce students to their Sloyd tools (talk about their care and safe use). Refresh students on how to use a ruler (note where "0" starts) and how to use the edge to draw a straight line.

entries.title: Term Prep & Teacher Tips
entries.content:
Sloyd: Level 1
Teachers need to watch "Sloyd Introduction" before the first lesson and complete the reading assignment in order to understand the purpose and process of Sloyd. (See Quick Links below.)

blocks.text: Lessons will go much more smoothly if you prepare thoroughly. As students gain experience, develop fine motor skills, and practice habits of precision they will need less support.
Watch video tutorials or make the model yourself. Gather supplies and materials needed.
Connect back to previous lesson and skills learned.
Read the instructions one step at a time. Model each step and make sure students complete it with precision before moving to the next step.
When a project is complete, students may repeat it as many times as they wish to give as gifts or create original patterns similar to the project.
